commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=e4daae52edf954a8ccc1f9aceb197368aefaae85)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes embedded-agent model-call diagnostics to measure stream chunks with `partial` removed and adds regression tests for incremental delta counting plus opaque chunk inspection failures.

PR surface: Source +19, Tests +104. Total +123 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the diagnostics amplification path: current main serializes every streamed chunk, and provider stream adapters include growing `partial` snapshots on deltas. The full Windows local-provider starvation report was not rerun here.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining blocker is a maintainer compatibility/API decision for an exposed diagnostic metric, not a safe automated repair.

target.title: fix(agents): count stream deltas incrementally
target.body:
## Summary

- fixes the diagnostics stream-byte counter introduced by `c6e98493511b3a18e6f2f402693ef7dc832a071f` so plain stream deltas are counted from the new `delta` only
- avoids serializing full `partial` assistant snapshots on every streamed chunk, which removes the repeated answer-so-far work called out in https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/86599#issuecomment-4555471713
- keeps diagnostic byte inspection best-effort so opaque or hostile chunk shapes cannot break the model stream

Refs https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/86599.

## Verification

Behavior addressed: model-call diagnostics no longer repeatedly stringify full accumulated assistant partial snapshots for ordinary `text_delta`, `thinking_delta`, and `toolcall_delta` stream chunks.

Real environment tested: AWS Crabbox Linux changed gate against the rebased maintainer branch, focused local Vitest wrapper in a Codex worktree, formatter/diff checks, and branch autoreview.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```sh
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.test.ts --reporter=dot
node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.test.ts
git diff --check origin/main...HEAD
.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main
node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --idle-timeout 45m --ttl 90m --timing-json --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"
```

Evidence after fix: focused local wrapper passed `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.test.ts` with 13/13 tests after rebase at `11be0d1ca64`; oxfmt and diff checks passed; autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings; AWS Crabbox `check:changed` passed with provider `aws`, lease `cbx_ff6f51c1ddf6`, slug `violet-crab`, run `run_9b41fd763eb8`, machine `c7a.8xlarge`, exit 0, `leaseStopped=true`, lanes `core` and `coreTests`.

Observed result after fix: regression coverage proves diagnostic byte accounting does not call `toJSON` on full partial snapshots for text deltas, counts only incremental delta bytes, and keeps streaming alive when byte inspection cannot inspect a chunk.

What was not tested: Windows local-daemon reproduction from https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/86599 was not rerun in this PR. This removes the diagnostics amplification from the starvation cluster but does not claim to close every related local-provider responsiveness path alone.
target.author: vincentkoc
